title: Chaerophyllum nanhuense
taxonomy: Chaerophyllum nanhuense is a species of plant in the family Apiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to China. The plant belongs to the genus Chaerophyllum, which is commonly known as the Queen-Anne's-lace or wild chervil. Chaerophyllum nanhuense is further classified as part of the subfamily Apioideae. The exact classification within the family Apiaceae requires further research for a more precise explanation.

intro: Chaerophyllum nanhuense, also known as Nanhu angelica, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of China. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chaerophyllum nanhuense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
